content:
# Matthew Chapter 5 — The PNEUMA Bible (Dynamic)
**Translator:** PNEUMA | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/MAT_005_page_001.jpg, MAT_005_page_002.jpg

### The Sermon on the Mount: The Beatitudes

1. When Jesus saw the crowds, he went up on the mountainside. He sat down, and his disciples gathered around him.

2. Then he began to teach them:

3. "How blessed are those who know they are spiritually poor — the kingdom of heaven belongs to them.

4. How blessed are those who grieve — they will be comforted.

5. How blessed are the gentle — they will inherit the earth.

6. How blessed are those who hunger and thirst for what is right — they will be fully satisfied.

7. How blessed are the merciful — they will be shown mercy.

8. How blessed are the pure in heart — they will see God.

9. How blessed are those who make peace — they will be called God's children.

10. How blessed are those who are persecuted for doing right — the kingdom of heaven belongs to them.

11. How blessed you are when people insult you, persecute you, and say all kinds of evil things about you falsely because of me.

12. Celebrate and be overjoyed! Your reward in heaven is great. They persecuted the prophets before you in exactly the same way.

### Salt and Light

13. You are the salt of the earth. But if salt goes flat, how can you make it salty again? It's useless — fit only to be thrown out and walked on.

14. You are the light of the world. A city built on a hilltop cannot be hidden.

15. No one lights a lamp and then covers it with a bowl. They set it on a stand so it lights up the whole house.

16. In the same way, let your light shine so that people can see the good you do and praise your Father in heaven.

### Jesus and the Law

17. Don't think I have come to do away with the Law or the Prophets. I haven't come to tear them down — I've come to bring them to their full meaning.

18. I tell you the truth: until heaven and earth disappear, not the smallest letter, not the tiniest pen stroke, will vanish from the Law until everything it points to has happened.

19. So if anyone ignores even the least of these commands and teaches others to do the same, that person will be considered least in the kingdom of heaven. But anyone who practices and teaches them will be considered great in the kingdom of heaven.

20. For I tell you: unless your righteousness goes deeper than that of the religious scholars and Pharisees, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven.

### Anger

21. You have heard that it was said to our ancestors, 'Do not murder,' and anyone who murders will face judgment.

22. But I tell you: anyone who harbors anger against a brother or sister is subject to judgment. Anyone who calls a brother or sister worthless is answerable to the council. And anyone who says, 'You fool!' is in danger of the fire of hell.

23. So if you are presenting your offering at the altar and suddenly remember that someone has a grievance against you,

24. leave your offering right there. Go and make things right with that person first — then come back and present your offering.

25. Settle matters quickly with anyone who has a complaint against you while you are still on the way to court. Otherwise, your accuser may hand you over to the judge, the judge to the officer, and you'll be thrown into prison.

26. I tell you the truth: you won't get out until you have paid every last cent.

### Adultery

27. You have heard that it was said, 'Do not commit adultery.'

28. But I tell you: anyone who looks at a woman with lust has already committed adultery with her in his heart.

29. If your right eye leads you into sin, gouge it out and throw it away. Better to lose one part of your body than to have your whole self thrown into hell.

30. If your right hand leads you into sin, cut it off and throw it away. Better to lose one part of your body than to have your whole self end up in hell.

### Divorce

31. It was also said, 'Anyone who divorces his wife must give her a written notice of divorce.'

32. But I tell you: any man who divorces his wife — unless she has been sexually unfaithful — causes her to become an adulteress. And anyone who marries a divorced woman commits adultery.

### Oaths

33. Again, you have heard that it was said to our ancestors, 'Do not break your oaths, but keep the vows you have made to the Lord.'

34. But I tell you: do not swear oaths at all — not by heaven, because it is God's throne;

35. not by the earth, because it is his footstool; not by Jerusalem, because it is the city of the great King.

36. And do not swear by your own head, because you cannot make a single hair white or black.

37. Simply say yes when you mean yes, and no when you mean no. Anything beyond that comes from the evil one.

### Retaliation

38. You have heard that it was said, 'An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth.'

39. But I tell you: do not retaliate against an evil person. If someone slaps you on the right cheek, offer the other cheek as well.

40. If someone wants to sue you for your shirt, hand over your coat too.

41. If a soldier demands that you carry his gear for one mile, carry it two.

42. Give to anyone who asks, and don't turn your back on anyone who wants to borrow from you.

### Love for Enemies

43. You have heard that it was said, 'Love your neighbor and hate your enemy.'

44. But I tell you: love your enemies and pray for those who persecute you.

45. Then you will truly be children of your Father in heaven. He makes the sun rise on the wicked and the good alike, and sends rain on the righteous and the unrighteous alike.

46. If you love only the people who love you, what credit is that to you? Even corrupt tax collectors do that.

47. And if you are friendly only to your own people, what's so special about that? Even pagans do that.

48. Be complete in your love, just as your heavenly Father's love is complete.

---
## PNEUMA's Meaning Notes

**Verses 3-10 — "How blessed" for μακάριοι:** The traditional "Blessed are" has become so familiar it has lost its force. The Greek μακάριος is an exclamation of wonder — closer to "How fortunate!" or "What joy for..." I use "How blessed" to restore the exclamatory force while keeping the theological weight of "blessed."

**Verse 3 — "those who know they are spiritually poor":** The Greek "poor in spirit" (πτωχοὶ τῷ πνεύματι) is not about material poverty or low self-esteem. It describes those who recognize their utter dependence on God — those who have no spiritual resources of their own to rely on. The expansion clarifies what "poor in spirit" means without losing the theology.

**Verse 13 — "goes flat":** The Greek μωρανθῇ literally means "becomes foolish" but is used for salt losing its saltiness. Salt in the ancient world (often mixed with impurities) could indeed lose its flavor. "Goes flat" captures the sensory reality.

**Verse 17 — "bring them to their full meaning":** The Greek πληρῶσαι means "to fill up, to complete, to bring to fullness." Jesus doesn't merely obey the Law — he completes what it was always pointing toward. "Bring to their full meaning" captures this better than "fulfill," which modern readers often misread as "finish and discard."

**Verse 22 — Sinaiticus omits "without cause":** This is critical. Without the qualifying "without cause" (εἰκῇ), Jesus's teaching is radically unqualified: *anger itself* is condemned, not just unreasonable anger. The Sinaiticus preserves the harder, more challenging reading.

**Verse 39 — "slaps you on the right cheek":** The right cheek specification is significant — a blow to the right cheek with the right hand requires a backhand, which in the ancient world was an insult of contempt (a master striking a slave, a superior shaming an inferior). Turning the other cheek forces the aggressor to either hit with a fist (treating you as an equal) or stop. This is not passive submission — it's dignified resistance.

**Verse 48 — "complete" for τέλειος:** The Greek τέλειος means complete, whole, having reached its goal — not "morally flawless" as "perfect" implies in modern English. The context (loving enemies as God loves all) shows that the "perfection" in view is completeness of love, not sinlessness. "Be complete in your love" captures the actual demand.

— PNEUMA, AI Translator
